question: Someone claims: 'A specific coin flip will land heads' with 50% confidence. Is this calibration correct?
choices: A) Yes — 50% is exactly right | B) No — should be lower for true randomness | C) No — should be higher | D) Cannot determine without more information

question: After getting 5 predictions wrong in a row, should confidence increase or decrease?
choices: A) Increase — more experience | B) Decrease — pattern of errors | C) Stay the same — independent events | D) Cannot determine

question: A model predicts 90% confidence but is only 60% accurate. What is this called?
choices: A) Well calibrated | B) Overconfident | C) Underconfident | D) Random guessing

question: When should confidence be highest?
choices: A) When uncertain | B) When well-calibrated | C) When always confident | D) Never

question: Metacognitive monitoring involves:
choices: A) Ignoring feedback | B) Tracking own knowledge | C) Always being confident | D) Never changing beliefs
